case_title: MIRIAM GARCIA et al VS. ST. LUKES HOSPITAL et al

docket_entries.description: ANSWER TO COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS ST. LUKES HOSPITAL)
DECLARATION OF EXPERT ALBERT FERRARI IN SUPPORT OF PLTF'S OPPOS TO DEFTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF GARCIA, MIRIAM MILANO, JAVIER

OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED IN OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS ST. LUKES HOSPITAL)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS ST. LUKE'S HOSPITAL) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. (COURT MADE A PERSONAL DISCLOSURE. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE.) ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT HAD CONSTRUCTIVE NOTICE OF THE DANGEROUS CONDITION. THE OBJECTIONS TO THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF JAVIER MILANO ARE OVERRULED. JAVIER MILANO SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS A TIMELY LOSS OF CONSORTIUM CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR #12353
NOTICE OF NON-PAYMENT OF JURY FEES

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON NOV-22-2011 IN 206, PLAINTIFFS MIRIAM GARCIA AND JAVIER MILANO'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL IS GRANTED. THE TRIAL DATE OF 12/5/11 IS CONTINUED TO 5/14/12 IN DEPT. 206 AT 9:30 AM. THE CURRENT SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE OF 12/2/11 AT 1:30 PM IN DEPT. 613 IS MAINTAINED. ALL PARTIES STIPULATE TO CONDITIONS THAT ARE IDENTIFIED IN THE ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: E. BURA; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER (erroneously sued herein as St. Luke's Hospital) MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

APPEARANCES: Nelson C. Barry, III, Esq. & Agatha Panday, Esq. of Law Offices of Nelson C. Barry, III 587-0550 for pltfs & opposing parties; Alan J. Zacharin, Esq. of Law Offices Alan J. Zacharin 510/508-9702 for deft & moving party.

RULING: (Court made a personal disclosure. No objections were made.) Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Denied. There is a triable issue whether defendant had constructive notice of the dangerous condition. The objections to the deposition testimony of Javier Milano are overruled. Javier Milano sufficiently pleads a timely loss of consortium claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR #12353

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
